architecture_fit.note: Single attention block, no MLP, no learned weights — a genuine minimal delta that sorts via comparison-counting + rank routing.

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform 1/L baseline plotted and shown decaying; positional-shortcut strawman is argued via robustness but not run as an explicit model.

faithfulness.note: Correctly flags it as a synthetic hand-set circuit and supplies a real causal check — the τ-ablation softens the counting head and accuracy collapses to the uniform floor.

operating_range.note: sort_accuracy=1.0 and robustness=1.0 hold flat across the full L=4→32 sweep; mechanism is value-comparison based so length-invariant.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire circuit is hand-set (τ, β) with no training — exactly the hardcoded-weights case, reproducing target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Heatmap with argsort overlay, accuracy-vs-length against 1/L, and accuracy-vs-τ ablation together make the sorting claim checkable at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README cleanly maps each chart to a claim: heatmap=sort correctness, acc-vs-length=robustness, acc-vs-τ=mechanism ablation.

notes: A tight, hand-built single-attention-head circuit that sorts by counting comparisons into ranks then routing slot i to rank i, achieving perfect sort_accuracy and robustness across the full L=4→32 sweep. It is minimal-delta architecture with an excellent hardcoded-weights demonstration and a genuine τ-ablation standing in for the faithfulness check. The only soft spot is that the positional-shortcut strawman is argued rather than run as an explicit competing model.
